frontier: Smallest N that is the sum of three prime cubes in exactly 6 unordered ways (A385316 a(6)).
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Constructed and executed the bounded exact search to C=1e12: enumerated all nondecreasing triples (p≤q≤r) of primes with p^3≤C into their sums, counted exact multiplicities via Counter, and found zero values with multiplicity 6 in [0, 1e12].
verified:
Artifact: prime_cubes_a385316.py, SHA-256: 9221b3a8ba9217ea25baf3613eba044317d2f5945fc19e1010c0743f25c957b8
Domain kill fixtures: 2/2 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 1/1 rejected correctly
ALL KILL FIXTURES PASSED
Reproduce: a(1)=24, a(2)=185527, a(3)=8627527, a(4)=999979163, a(5)=10588881419 — all match OEIS A385316.
Search: C=1e12, 1229 primes, 235,275,700 total sums, 234,585,699 distinct. Multiplicity distribution: 2 values at multiplicity 5, 37 at 4, 4,074 at 3, 681,734 at 2, 233,899,852 at 1. Zero values at multiplicity 6.
result: Certified negative result: a(6) > 1e12. The exact bounded search to C=1e12 completed in 133.5 seconds on the Mac. No number ≤1e12 has exactly 6 unordered representations as a sum of three prime cubes. The prior literature lower bound was a(6) > ~4.99e11; this extends the certified lower bound to 1e12.
next_gate: Raise the search cutoff to C=1e13 (≈11,700 primes, ~1.7B triples, estimated ~1,800s on Mac) or dispatch to DGX for the same. At C=1e13, if no multiplicity-6 value is found, extend further until the limsup conjecture is either witness (a 6-representation found) or a much larger bound is certified.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training.
